title: Consumer Policy
summary: This Treasury Department page describes the Office of Consumer Policy's mission to promote financial literacy, education, and consumer access to credit through the Financial Literacy and Education Commission (FLEC). The content covers administrative matters such as a February 2026 FLEC public meeting about Trump Accounts and financial education initiatives, along with links to annual reports and national strategy documents.
key_points: Office of Consumer Policy (OCP) coordinates federal financial literacy efforts through the FLEC, chaired by the Treasury Secretary
FLEC held a public meeting on February 6, 2026 featuring discussion of Trump Accounts implementation and youth financial education
Annual reports to Congress for FY 2023-2024 and FY 2025 document FLEC activities and federal financial literacy priorities
The National Strategy for Financial Literacy 2020 outlines federal priorities for consumer financial decision-making
Page is a navigation/listing page linking to multiple documents rather than containing a specific analysis or data release
why_it_matters: This source is not relevant to macro catalysts or market structure. It covers government consumer financial education programs and administrative commission activities, which do not provide market-moving information, policy signals, or economic data that would inform trading or investment decisions.
